---
title: "établi lattttice"
sidebar: en
toc: true
toc-depth: 2
---


> **lattttice** is four-dimensional tic-tac-toe on a 4×4×4×4 board: 256 cells,
> 4 in a row to win, along any line through four dimensions. Because no screen
> is 4D, lattttice gives you four ways to *see* the same position — a grid of
> grids, axis slices, a 3D Schlegel projection, and a strength heat-map — plus
> live win-probability analysis and an AI opponent.
> It's for puzzle and abstract-strategy players who want tic-tac-toe taken to an
> absurd, beautiful extreme.
>
> **Stack:** Expo (React Native) + TypeScript, Zustand state, react-native-skia
> charts, expo-gl for the 3D projection. Part of the **établi** suite. Every
> figure is a real screenshot of the v0.1.0 release build on an Android emulator,
> regenerated by `scripts/capture.sh`.

## Table of contents
1. [Quick start](#quick-start)
2. [Grid of grids](#feature-grid-of-grids)
3. [Playing moves](#feature-playing-moves)
4. [Slices view](#feature-slices-view)
5. [3D Schlegel projection](#feature-3d-schlegel-projection)
6. [Heat view](#feature-heat-view)
7. [Win-probability analysis](#feature-win-probability-analysis)
8. [Modes, difficulty & reset](#feature-modes-difficulty--reset)
9. [Showcase / gallery](#showcase--gallery)
10. [Reproducing these figures](#reproducing-these-figures)
11. [Known gaps](#known-gaps)
12. [Version](#version)

## Quick start
1. Install the v0.1.0 APK (`lattttice-0.1.0.apk`) and open it. It cold-starts to an empty 4×4×4×4 board in the **Grid** view, "X to move".

   ![Home — the empty 4D board as a grid of grids](../assets/0.1.0/01-home-grid.png)

2. Four tabs change how you *see* the board: **Grid · Slices · 3D · Heat**. The same position is shown in each.

## Feature: Grid of grids
The default view lays the hypercube out as a 4×4 arrangement of 4×4 boards. Each sub-board is labelled `z·w` (the two "outer" dimensions); within it you see the 4×4 of x,y cells. All 256 cells are visible at once.

![Grid view — 16 sub-boards labelled z0·w0 … z3·w3](../assets/0.1.0/01-home-grid.png)

## Feature: Playing moves
Tap any empty cell to drop the side to move. X is blue, O is amber. The header flips to the next player and the analysis begins recomputing ("analysing…").

![A few moves placed across different sub-boards](../assets/0.1.0/02-moves-grid.png)

## Feature: Slices view
**Slices** isolates one `w` layer at a time (a `w =` selector at the top) and shows its four `z` boards larger — easier to read a single 3D slab of the 4D space.

![Slices view — w-selector and the z0 slice with a stone](../assets/0.1.0/03-slices.png)

## Feature: 3D Schlegel projection
**3D** renders a read-only Schlegel projection of the hypercube — all 256 cells as a depth-shaded point cloud, with your stones positioned in 3D so you can see lines that cut across dimensions. (You still play from the 2D views; this one is for spatial intuition.)

![3D Schlegel projection of the 4D board](../assets/0.1.0/04-projection3d.png)

## Feature: Heat view
**Heat** shades every empty cell by move strength for the side to move, on a viridis scale (the legend shows the value range). Bright cells are the strong moves; it turns the 256-cell board into a readable pressure map.

![Heat view — every empty cell shaded by strength](../assets/0.1.0/05-heat.png)

## Feature: Win-probability analysis
Below the board, a react-native-skia chart plots P(X) / P(O) / P(draw) across the game and calls out the **turning point** (the ply with the biggest swing). It updates as you play.

![Analysis — win-probability chart and turning point](../assets/0.1.0/06-analysis-settings.png)

## Feature: Modes, difficulty & reset
Settings sit beneath the analysis: **Mode** (Hotseat / vs Computer), and when vs Computer is selected, an **AI difficulty** (Easy / Medium / Hard). Per-player **strength-hint** overlays can be toggled, and **New Game** clears the board.

![vs Computer mode with AI difficulty](../assets/0.1.0/07-vs-computer.png)

Resetting returns to a fresh, empty board:

![Fresh board after New Game](../assets/0.1.0/08-reset.png)

## Showcase / gallery
| | | |
|---|---|---|
| ![](../assets/0.1.0/01-home-grid.png) | ![](../assets/0.1.0/03-slices.png) | ![](../assets/0.1.0/04-projection3d.png) |
| grid of grids | slices | 3D projection |
| ![](../assets/0.1.0/05-heat.png) | ![](../assets/0.1.0/06-analysis-settings.png) | ![](../assets/0.1.0/07-vs-computer.png) |
| heat map | analysis | vs computer |

## Reproducing these figures
```bash
# 1. build + install the release APK on a booted emulator/device
npx expo run:android --variant release
# 2. regenerate every figure into vignettes/../assets/0.1.0/
bash scripts/capture.sh
```
Device settings for deterministic capture: 1080×2400 @ 420dpi, animations
disabled. The 3D view is GL-rendered; the harness allows it extra settle time
before capture. Slugs in `scripts/capture.sh` map 1:1 to the filenames above.

> **Build note:** the release build needs a larger Gradle heap than the Expo
> default (`org.gradle.jvmargs=-Xmx6144m` in `android/gradle.properties`) because
> of the native dependency set (skia + gl + reanimated + screens). The default
> 2 GB heap OOMs at `:app:collectReleaseDependencies`.

## Known gaps
- **Winning line / win banner:** not captured for v0.1.0. Forcing a specific
  4-in-a-row through 4D via scripted blind taps is unreliable; this state is
  reachable in play but was left as a documented gap rather than faked. A
  seeded "won position" hook would let the harness capture it deterministically
  (planned).

## Version
Documents établi **lattttice v0.1.0** (applicationId `com.raban.lattttice`,
versionCode 1). Part of the établi (workbench) suite.
